name: Alfred Hitchcock

acts.description: Obsessive psychological manipulation and harassment of Tippi Hedren, using his power to destroy her career when she refused him
Pattern of controlling, obsessive behavior toward multiple actresses in his films

behavior_summary: Master of suspense who controlled and sexually harassed his leading ladies, most systematically Tippi Hedren, whose career he deliberately sabotaged when she rejected him.

art.summary: The Master of Suspense. Psycho and Vertigo are among the most studied films ever made. His influence on cinema is incalculable.

verdict.blurb: He made Vertigo — a film literally about a man's obsessive need to control a woman into his ideal image — while doing exactly that to Tippi Hedren in real life. The film is about him. He cast himself as Jimmy Stewart and thought no one would notice.
